disclosure.findings:
CURRENT MECHANISM. The Skill tool loads /Users/charlie.hall/Workspaces/cortex-command/skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md in full (25,045 bytes) on every invocation. RESUME-AT-PLAN PATH executes: Project Configuration (SKILL.md:39-41, 218B); Step 1 argument parse + backlog resolver (SKILL.md:43-69, ~3.5KB — resolver routing at :51-69 runs whenever $ARGUMENTS is non-empty, so it IS resume-relevant); Step 2 detect-phase, criticality/tier detection, session registration, staleness signals (SKILL.md:71-115, ~4.3KB); Backlog Status Check + Backlog Write-Back from backlog-writeback.md (lines 5-35, 73-95, ~3.1KB of the 6,008B file); init drift refresh (SKILL.md:126-128, 335B); Step 3 skip-delegation branch (SKILL.md:136, ~290B) + phase table (:167-174); Reference-path propagation (SKILL.md:176-182, 1,845B — needed because the session auto-advances into Implement/Review); Phase Transition + Per-phase completion rules + Kept-pauses runtime backstop (SKILL.md:184-226, ~5.5KB); then references/plan.md (26,360B).

RESUME-DEAD CONTENT loaded today: refine-delegation procedure steps 1-6 incl. event-log JSON templates (SKILL.md:140-163, ~3.4KB); prose-slug derivation (SKILL.md:47, ~0.7KB — but cross-referenced by skills/refine/SKILL.md:39, which reads "lifecycle SKILL.md Step 1" verbatim); backlog-writeback.md's "Create index.md (New Lifecycle Only)" section (lines 37-71, ~2.9KB); the ENTIRE discovery-bootstrap.md (3,119B — its first line scopes it to "phase = research", yet SKILL.md:119-124 unconditionally instructs reading both reference files and running all four sub-procedures in order); misc (Contents listing, Lifecycle Directory section, ~0.8KB). Total resume-at-plan load today ≈ 60.5KB (25,045 + 6,008 + 3,119 + 26,360), of which ~10-12KB is dead on this path. Pre-phase overhead (everything except plan.md) is 34.2KB; ~30% of it is path-dead.

FRESH-FEATURE PATH additionally executes prose-slug derivation, index.md creation, lifecycle-slug write-back (backlog-writeback.md:85-91), Discovery Bootstrap, and the full refine delegation — which mandates a verbatim read of skills/refine/SKILL.md (21,236B, SKILL.md:142) plus complexity-escalation.md (1,138B) and post-refine-commit.md (7,031B). OVERNIGHT RUNNER: verified it NEVER loads skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md. Workers are driven by cortex_command/pipeline/prompts/implement.md and review.md (feature_executor.py:70 loads IMPLEMENT_TEMPLATE via importlib.resources from cortex_command.pipeline.prompts); plan generation is driven by cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md, which embeds its own protocol and cites skills/lifecycle/references/plan.md §1a/§1b only as documented precedent (orchestrator-round.md:242, :302); report.py:965 cites references/review.md §4a in a docstring. COUPLINGS THAT CONSTRAIN ANY RESTRUCTURE: (1) tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py parses the "Kept user pauses" section out of SKILL.md (test line 26) and scans every AskUserQuestion mention under skills/lifecycle/ and skills/refine/ with ±20-line anchors — the inventory entry "SKILL.md:60" (ambiguous-resolver pause) and any new reference file containing the string AskUserQuestion will need inventory updates. options.name: (a) Thin-router SKILL.md with per-path references (new-feature-bootstrap.md / resume.md)
options.mechanism: Shrink SKILL.md to a router: frontmatter, invocation, Step 1 parse + resolver, Step 2 detect-phase + routing table + paused/criticality/tier/session/staleness, init drift, and a fork instruction with a body-resolved path manifest. Route phase∈{none, research, specify} (and the spec-without-research inconsistent state) to references/new-feature-bootstrap.md (refine delegation steps, index.md creation, discovery bootstrap, lifecycle-slug write-back); route phase∈{plan, implement, implement-rework, review, complete, escalated} to references/resume.md (skip-announce, phase table, reference-path propagation usage, phase-transition + completion rules). Shared execution content (transition rules, completion gates) must live in resume.md and be read by the fresh path too after refine completes, since fresh sessions continue into Plan.
options.est_savings: Resume-at-plan: ~49-50KB vs 60.5KB today (~11KB, ~18%); pre-phase overhead drops 34.2KB → ~23KB. Router ~13KB + resume.md ~7KB + writeback slice ~3.1KB + plan.md 26.4KB.
options.risks: Highest blast radius. (1) Fresh path gets WORSE: +2 read hops (bootstrap ref, then resume.md after refine) and the router/bootstrap split must not orphan refine SKILL.md path resolution — all ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} targets the bootstrap file needs must be pre-resolved in the router body per ADR-0009. (2) Routing-table semantics for implement-rework (re-enter Implement), escalated (present reviewer analysis + ask user), and the -paused suffix strip currently live as one-liners in Step 2's table — splitting risks dropping the escalated/paused behavior from whichever file the agent actually reads. (3) phase=specify is a 'resume' by detect-phase but needs the refine-delegation path — naming the files new-vs-resume invites mis-routing; the real split is pre-plan vs plan-onward. (4) Parity test inventory anchors and the SKILL.md:60 entry move substantially; kept-pauses inventory location decision (router vs resume.md) forces a test change. (5) Two path files sharing the resolver/propagation idiom create drift surface. Savings over option (b) are only ~2KB because most of the router+core content is genuinely shared.

options.name: (b) Surgical extraction: refine-delegation block + new-lifecycle-only sub-procedures behind conditional reads
options.mechanism: Three moves, no routing change. (1) Extract SKILL.md:140-163 (delegation steps 1-6 incl. event-log JSON templates, ~3.4KB) to references/refine-delegation.md; the body keeps the three-way gate at :136-140 and replaces the steps with one line: 'read ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/references/refine-delegation.md and follow it', adding the file's ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} targets (refine SKILL.md, discovery-bootstrap, complexity-escalation, post-refine-commit) to the existing Reference-path propagation manifest at :176-182. (2) Make the discovery-bootstrap.md read conditional: body instructs reading it only when phase = none/research (it is already self-scoped to that phase at its line 3). (3) Split backlog-writeback.md's 'Create index.md (New Lifecycle Only)' section (lines 37-71, ~2.9KB) into a new-lifecycle reference (or merge into discovery-bootstrap's conditional read), leaving a ~3.1KB resume-relevant writeback file (Status Check + Write-Back). Step 2's detect-phase table, paused rule, escalated handling, staleness signals, kept-pauses inventory, and refine's Step 1 cross-pointer all stay in the body untouched.
options.est_savings: Resume-at-plan: ~51.5KB vs 60.5KB today (~9KB, ~15%); pre-phase overhead drops 34.2KB → ~25KB (~27%). SKILL.md ≈ 22.1KB (−3.4KB +0.4KB manifest growth), writeback slice 3.1KB, discovery-bootstrap 0, plan.md unchanged.
options.risks: Low. Fresh path gains exactly 1 read hop loading content it must read anyway (negligible against its existing ~75KB+, including the mandated 21.2KB refine SKILL.md verbatim read). No detect-phase edge-case exposure — implement-rework/escalated/paused routing is untouched in the body. Parity test: the SKILL.md:60 inventory anchor shifts by only a few lines (within ±20 tolerance after one inventory-line update); the extracted refine-delegation.md contains no AskUserQuestion sites. Residual risk: the extraction relies on the agent honoring the conditional ('read only when spec.md absent') — prose-gated, same enforcement class as the existing 'Read only the reference for the current phase' instruction at SKILL.md:174, which empirically holds. Captures ~80% of (a)'s savings at a fraction of the risk.

options.name: (c) Phase-keyed conditional-read table (option b formalized as structural routing)
options.mechanism: Same file moves as (b), but instead of scattering prose conditionals ('when phase = none also read X'), Step 2/3 gains ONE dispatch table keyed on detect-phase output that names exactly which body-resolved references to read for each phase value: none/research/specify → refine-delegation.md + new-lifecycle.md (+discovery-bootstrap.md when phase=none); plan→plan.md; implement & implement-rework→implement.md; review→review.md; complete→complete.md; escalated→inline body instructions; -paused→strip suffix then look up. This merges today's two separate gates (Step 2 routing table + Step 3 artifact-existence branch) into a single structural fork, aligning with the repo's 'structural separation over prose-only enforcement' principle (CLAUDE.md). The spec-without-research inconsistency check remains a one-line guard above the table.
options.est_savings: Same bytes as (b) on resume (~51.5KB, ~9KB saved) — the win over (b) is reliability, not bytes: a single table eliminates the two-gate redundancy where detect-phase says 'plan' but the agent re-derives the same answer from artifact existence at Step 3.
options.risks: Moderate-low. Collapsing the Step 3 artifact-existence branch into detect-phase routing assumes the detector's output is always consistent with artifact state — it is, by construction (the detector keys on the same artifacts), but the inconsistent spec-without-research warning at SKILL.md:138 keys on a state the detector maps to 'specify', so the table must carry that warning explicitly or it silently vanishes. Slightly larger SKILL.md diff than (b) (Step 2 table rewrite), so parity-test anchors and the refine cross-pointer need a verification pass. Fresh path: same single added hop as (b).

disclosure.recommendation: Recommend option (b), with (c)'s single-table formalization as an optional second commit if the two-gate redundancy bothers you in practice. Reasoning: the byte analysis shows SKILL.md's resume-irrelevant content is only ~10-12KB, and most of the rest (resolver routing, detect-phase table, transition rules, completion gates, reference-path propagation, kept-pauses backstop) is genuinely shared between paths — so option (a)'s thin router buys only ~2KB more than (b) while taking on the real risks: dropped escalated/implement-rework/paused semantics during the split, a misleading new-vs-resume naming (phase=specify resumes into the refine path), two extra fresh-path hops, a forced parity-test restructure, and a broken refine SKILL.md:39 cross-pointer unless separately patched. Option (b) extracts exactly the path-dead bytes (refine-delegation steps, index.md creation, discovery-bootstrap read) behind conditions that already exist in the text, touches no routing semantics, keeps the kept-pauses inventory and prose-slug prescription in the body where the parity test and refine's pointer expect them, and degrades the fresh path by one read hop on content it reads regardless. The overnight runner is unaffected by any option — it never loads SKILL.md (drives phases via cortex_command/pipeline/prompts/implement.md + review.md and cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md) — so the only consumers of this restructure are interactive sessions, which is exactly where the resume-at-plan pattern lives. Implementation notes for (b): add refine-delegation.md's transitive ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} targets to the body's Reference-path propagation section per ADR-0009 (cortex-check-skill-path will gate this), update the one kept-pauses inventory line for the SKILL.md:60 anchor shift, and run tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py before committing.

(2) Entries whose rationale contains 'phase-exit pause' (currently complete.md:73) are instead validated against a '### Step N' heading within ±35 lines. (3) Entries whose rationale contains 'conditional pause' (currently implement.md:49) additionally require a structural marker — read_branch_mode OR lifecycle_config OR cortex-lifecycle-branch-mode — within ±35 lines of the anchor. (4) REVERSE direction: every 'AskUserQuestion' line anywhere under skills/lifecycle/ and skills/refine/ *.md (19 sites today; SKILL.md's own '## Phase Transition' H2 section is excluded as prose) must have an inventory anchor within ±35 lines. Current inventory anchors: SKILL.md:60, clarify.md:57, specify.md:36/67/155, plan.md:277, implement.md:49, backlog-writeback.md:11, complete.md:73, refine SKILL.md:166. (1) TESTS: ~25 test modules read these files directly. The keystone is tests/test_lifecycle_kept_pauses_parity.py: bidirectional parity between the '### Kept user pauses' inventory in skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md (bullets of the form '- `file`:line — rationale') and actual 'AskUserQuestion' lines across both skill trees, with LINE_TOLERANCE = 35 (the docstring's ±20 is stale); 'phase-exit pause' entries validate against '### Step N' headings, 'conditional pause' entries also need a read_branch_mode/lifecycle_config/cortex-lifecycle-branch-mode marker within ±35 lines, and the reverse direction requires every AskUserQuestion site (19 today) to have an inventory anchor within ±35 lines — so any trim that shifts lines >35 or touches the section headings requires rewriting the inventory's file:line anchors in the same commit. Beyond that: implement.md is the most heavily pinned file (step-v ordered-token block, Branch selection block, branch-mode dispatch wiring, daytime-free negative pin, worktree-interactive contract, EnterWorktree ±60-line co-location); complete.md carries ~40 verbatim substrings, a byte-equality snapshot of the '**Hard guard**:' paragraph against tests/fixtures/complete_md_hard_guard.txt, and the '<!-- finalization-commit-step -->' marker region; review.md §4a, plan.md/review.md placeholders, load-requirements.md protocol shape, fanout.md 2×4 numeric table, refine SKILL.md §4 gate + two CLI wiring literals with ordering, clarify-critic.md verbatim prompt-template anchors, and SKILL.md's 50-line-distance post-refine-commit wiring. tests/test_lifecycle_references_resolve.py makes file:line citations FROM cortex/research/** INTO skill files hard-fail when trims push cited lines past EOF. Frontmatter is pinned by trigger-phrase/path-token fixtures and the discovery_source handoff token. (2) LINTS/HOOKS (.githooks/pre-commit, enabled via just setup-githooks): check-parity (E002/W003 — don't orphan bin/cortex-* scripts by deleting their last wiring mention), check-contract (E101/E103 — inline/fenced cortex-* invocations must carry required flags/subcommands; placeholders count; '<!-- contract-lint:ignore-next -->' sentinel), events-registry ('\"event\": \"name\"' literals must stay registered), prescriptive-prose, bare-python-import, and skill-path SP001/SP002 (keep SUBAGENT PROMPT fences paired and ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/ prefixes on Read/execute paths). The claude/hooks/cortex-skill-edit-advisor.sh PostToolUse hook re-runs validate-skill/callgraph + skill-design suites on every interactive edit. (3) DUAL-SOURCE MIRROR: pre-commit Phases 2–4 run `just build-plugin` (rsync skills/lifecycle and skills/refine into plugins/cortex-core/skills/) and block the commit on `git diff --quiet plugins/cortex-core/` drift — canonical + regenerated mirror must be committed together (cannot be deferred); pytest byte-parity tests are the --no-verify backstop. (4) CROSS-FILE CITATIONS: research/discovery/refine skills consult fanout.md (count matrix + hybrid-angle-selection section), load-requirements.md, lifecycle clarify.md/specify.md (§ numbers, '## Hard Gate', 'Research Sufficiency Criteria') at runtime — section names and § numbering are de-facto public API; docs (interactive-phases, skills-reference, agentic-layer, overnight-operations, internals/sdk), CLAUDE.md, and cortex/requirements cite paths/sections with no automated gate. (5) OVERNIGHT: the runner never reads these skill files headless — it uses package-internal prompts — but couples by contract: orchestrator-round.md cites plan.md §1b's findings-json LAST-occurrence envelope and task format, and Python (mark_task_done_in_plan, detect_lifecycle_phase, dashboard pr.json reader, report.py review-§4a mirror) parses artifacts whose grammar is prescribed by plan.md/complete.md prose, so trimming those format definitions desynchronizes prose from parser. (6) SESSIONSTART scan-lifecycle: references the skill only by invocation name (/cortex-core:lifecycle) — safe for prose trims.

metricsFix.findings: FIX 1 — TIER ATTRIBUTION. Current mechanism: cortex_command/pipeline/metrics.py:229-230 sets `tier = start_events[0]["tier"] if start_events else None` inside extract_feature_metrics — it takes the FIRST lifecycle_start's tier, ignores all complexity_override events, ignores later lifecycle_start re-seeds, and uses bracket access (latent KeyError if a lifecycle_start lacks "tier"; 0 occurrences in current data). That tier flows to format_feature_record (metrics.py:923, emitted as features.<name>.tier in metrics.json) and to compute_aggregates (metrics.py:977-981, groups by m["tier"]), which feeds compute_calibration (metrics.py:1067-1125) and the SessionStart summary (cortex_command/hooks/scan_lifecycle.py:505-513 reads aggregates.simple/.complex avg_task_count and avg_rework_cycles). CANONICAL RULE: cortex_command/common.py:660-738 reduce_lifecycle_state — lifecycle_start seeds tier (line 714-719), complexity_override supersedes via its `to` field only (lines 727-733), values gated by TIER_VOCABULARY={"simple","complex"} (line 619); read_tier (common.py:585-606) projects it with default "simple". Precedent: overnight exit report already uses read_tier (cortex_command/overnight/report.py:590), so metrics.py is the outlier reader. DATA IMPACT: 63 of 237 completed features (27%) would move bucket (all simple→complex; 79 live+archive logs contain complexity_override, every sampled one is from:simple to:complex). CONSUMERS OF CURRENT SEMANTICS: (a) SessionStart metrics line (scan_lifecycle.py:453-514) — simple/complex n and averages will visibly shift; (b) calibration insights strings in metrics.json (metrics.py:1067-1125); (c) dashboard slow-flag baselines (cortex_command/dashboard/data.py:1224-1235 filters features by entry.get("tier"), compared against the running feature's tier from dispatch_start complexity at data.py:1188 — dispatch complexity is post-override, so the fix REMOVES an existing inconsistency for escalated features); (d) docs/overnight-operations.md:351-367 rollback monitoring is UNAFFECTED — it reads model_tier_dispatch_aggregates whose tier comes from dispatch_start.complexity (metrics.py:434), already post-override at dispatch time; (e) morning-review skill does NOT read metrics.json (no references found in skills/morning-review/). TEST COVERAGE: only one existing test exercises extract_feature_metrics + compute_aggregates — cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_metrics.py:1573 (test_compute_aggregates_phase_durations_segmented_by_merge_anchor, lifecycle_start tier=simple at line 1615, no overrides — unaffected by the fix). No test covers override supersession; new tests required. FIX 2 — FLOAT FORMATTING. cortex_command/hooks/scan_lifecycle.py:492-503 (_fmt) emits floats via json.dumps, so avg_task_count=9.885714285714286 appears verbatim in the SessionStart line built at lines 510-514; the docstring at lines 461-467 explicitly codifies "no rounding" as bash-parity rationale, but the bash precedent is now a 9-line thin wrapper (hooks/cortex-scan-lifecycle.sh delegates to Python), so the parity constraint is dead — single-site fix. Golden fixtures tests/fixtures/hooks/scan_lifecycle/b_single_incomplete_feature.expected.additionalContext.txt:3 and d_post_clear_session_migration.expected.additionalContext.txt:3 pin "avg 0 tasks" (int defaults) — they stay byte-identical only if the int path in _fmt is preserved and rounding applies to floats alone; an unconditional f"{:.1f}" would turn "0" into "0.0" and force fixture regeneration (tests at tests/test_hooks_scan_lifecycle.py:307 and :343). No test directly covers _metrics_summary_line with float aggregates; one must be added. COUPLING FLAG: at scoping start, cortex_command/common.py and tests/test_reduce_lifecycle_state.py had uncommitted edits from another live session; mid-scoping that session committed them (commits a7fac1bf merge, 25782ca4 "Complete cortex-lifecycle-state-collapses-to-null (#287)", 020cbb5c) — all common.py line cites above are from the now-committed HEAD state, but any option touching common.py must still coordinate with that session (per-pathspec commits on shared main) and note CLAUDE.md requires a lifecycle for common.py edits.

options.name: Option A (fix 1): inline post-override fold in extract_feature_metrics
options.mechanism: Replace metrics.py:229-230 with a ~6-line fold over the in-memory events list mirroring the canonical rule: lifecycle_start seeds tier (later starts re-seed), last complexity_override's `to` wins; .get() access kills the latent KeyError. Add a comment cross-referencing common.py reduce_lifecycle_state plus a parity test asserting the fold agrees with read_tier on a shared fixture. Changes: extract_feature_metrics (metrics.py:198-284); new tests in cortex_command/pipeline/tests/test_metrics.py (override supersedes, last-override-wins, no-override unchanged, parity-vs-read_tier); existing test at :1573 untouched.
options.est_savings: Smallest diff (~10 lines code + ~60 lines tests); no common.py lifecycle requirement; no concurrent-session coordination; fixes 63/237 misattributed features immediately.
options.risks: Duplicates the canonical rule outside common.py, contradicting the 'all reader sites agree by construction' intent (common.py:521-524, 578-580); drift risk is real but bounded by the parity test. Skips TIER_VOCABULARY enforcement (current data is all in-vocab, so no behavioral gap today).

options.name: Option B (fix 1): reuse reduce_lifecycle_state(Path) at extract_all_feature_metrics
options.mechanism: After extract_feature_metrics returns, overwrite m['tier'] with reduce_lifecycle_state(log_path).state.get('tier') in extract_all_feature_metrics (metrics.py:840-866). Zero rule duplication; canonical reducer verbatim.
options.est_savings: ~4 lines; canonical by construction; vocabulary enforcement for free.
options.risks: Double file read per log (trivial); splits tier semantics across two functions — unit callers of extract_feature_metrics (the existing test seam at test_metrics.py:1653) silently keep the old buggy semantics, which is a trap for future tests; out-of-vocab historical tiers would become None and drop out of aggregates (none exist today, but it is a silent behavior change).

options.name: Option C (fix 1): factor a pure-events reducer into common.py and have metrics call it
options.mechanism: Extract the event-fold body of reduce_lifecycle_state (common.py:687-738) into reduce_lifecycle_events(records) operating on parsed dicts; Path version delegates; extract_feature_metrics calls it on its in-memory events. Single source of truth for every reader including the in-memory case. Changes: common.py refactor (behavior-preserving), extract_feature_metrics, tests in test_metrics.py, possibly a thin test in tests/test_reduce_lifecycle_state.py.
options.est_savings: Durable version per the repo's solution-horizon principle — eliminates the duplication Option A accepts and the split-semantics trap Option B creates; the same shared fold is the already-planned direction of #287's reducer consolidation.
options.risks: common.py edits require a lifecycle per CLAUDE.md and that exact file was being modified by a concurrent session until mid-scoping (now committed as #287) — coordinate before touching; largest blast radius of the three; vocabulary enforcement changes null-tier handling as in Option B.

options.name: Option D (fix 1 addendum): report BOTH initial and final tier
options.mechanism: Whichever resolution option is chosen, additionally keep the lifecycle_start tier as initial_tier in extract_feature_metrics' return dict (metrics.py:273-284) and format_feature_record (metrics.py:922-933). Aggregates still must pick one tier — the bug IS the aggregate attribution, so 'both' is not an alternative to changing semantics, only an additive provenance field (~5 lines).
options.est_savings: Near-zero cost; preserves historical comparability and makes escalation rate queryable from metrics.json (initial != tier).
options.risks: Schema addition consumers must tolerate (dashboard parse_metrics at data.py:1124-1138 passes dicts through, tolerant); dashboard/seed.py:405-460 synthetic metrics.json would lack the field unless updated — harmless since all readers use .get().

options.name: Option E (fix 2): float-only rounding in _fmt
options.mechanism: In scan_lifecycle.py _fmt (lines 492-503), branch floats to f"{val:.1f}" (ints keep json.dumps passthrough so the golden fixtures' 'avg 0 tasks' stays byte-identical); update the docstring at lines 461-467 that codifies no-rounding bash parity (the bash precedent is now a 9-line delegating wrapper, so the constraint is dead). Add one unit test feeding _metrics_summary_line a metrics.json with avg_task_count=9.885714285714286 asserting 'avg 9.9 tasks'. Golden fixtures b/d and their tests (tests/test_hooks_scan_lifecycle.py:307, :343) unchanged.
options.est_savings: ~3-line code change + ~20-line test; removes 13 junk digits from every SessionStart context injection.
options.risks: If rounding is applied unconditionally instead of float-only, golden fixtures break ('0' -> '0.0') and need regeneration; _safe_mean always returns floats, so a true average of 4 renders '4.0' not '4' — cosmetic, acceptable. Note scan_lifecycle.py sits under cortex_command/hooks/, not the root hooks/ dir, so the CLAUDE.md lifecycle-required path list arguably does not cover it, but treating it as hook code under a simple-tier lifecycle is the safe read.

metricsFix.recommendation: Bundle both fixes as one simple-tier lifecycle. For fix 1, take Option A (inline fold + parity test against read_tier) now, with Option D's initial_tier field added since it costs ~5 lines and preserves historical comparability — and explicitly defer Option C as the durable consolidation if/when common.py is next opened, since #287's reducer work (just committed by the concurrent session) is the natural home and touching common.py today means a lifecycle gate plus coordination with that session. Option B is the worst of both: it splits tier semantics across the unit seam that the only existing extract_feature_metrics test (test_metrics.py:1653) exercises. Do not change semantics-only-by-adding-fields: aggregates must group by final effective tier because that is the bug (63/237 = 27% of completed features are misattributed, and the dashboard slow-flag comparison at data.py:1188/1229 is currently comparing post-override dispatch tier against pre-override feature tier — the fix resolves that inconsistency rather than creating one). Expect and announce the number shift in the SessionStart metrics line and calibration insights; the overnight rollback-monitoring docs are unaffected (dispatch aggregates already use post-override complexity) and morning-review does not read metrics.json. For fix 2, Option E exactly as scoped: float-only rounding inside _fmt plus docstring update and one new unit test; the int path must be preserved so golden fixtures b/d remain byte-identical. Implementation note from project memory: edit cortex_command on main with sequential dispatch (not a worktree) since `just test` runs the editable install, and commit with explicit pathspecs while the other live session shares the checkout.

Lifecycle 1 (complex tier): (i) verified prose trim of the 12 mapped files applying only the 158 safe proposals plus the 37 downgrades in downgraded form, never the 36 unsafe (~36.5KB per result.safeTotalBytes=36539); (ii) dedup options 1-4 from result.duplication.recommendation (~9-10KB: shared critical-review-gate.md reference replacing specify.md:165-180/plan.md:273-288, verification-gates.md restatement trims in critical-review SKILL.md, cortex-lifecycle-state read protocol canonicalized in criticality-matrix.md with canonical homes reachable from lifecycle+refine+discovery+research per critic gap 2, micro-dedups), explicitly skipping dup options 5 (approval-surface, kept-pauses-coupled) and 6 (auto-advance reinforcements, event JSON shapes, model-routing values, dispatched-verbatim templates); (iii) progressive disclosure OPTION (B) ONLY — surgical extraction of refine-delegation block + conditional discovery-bootstrap read + backlog-writeback new-lifecycle split — matching the disclosure agent's own recommendation (~9KB resume-path savings, lowest blast radius, no routing-semantics change); (iv) a cheap sync of skills/discovery/references/orchestrator-review.md with the lifecycle copy's trims (critic gap 3: 79 diff lines, trimming one side entrenches drift). Lifecycle 2 (simple tier, separate small PR): metrics Option A (inline post-override fold in metrics.py:229-230 + parity test vs read_tier) + Option D (initial_tier provenance field) + Option E (float-only rounding in scan_lifecycle.py _fmt preserving the int path so golden fixtures b/d stay byte-identical), with the 63/237 re-bucketing surfaced as an explicit Changes-to-Existing-Behavior spec item for user approval. MECHANISM: (b), defer (c) — (c) buys zero bytes over (b) per the evidence ('the win is reliability, not bytes'), changes routing semantics (merges Step 2/3 gates), and risks silently dropping the spec-without-research warning; ship it later only if the two-gate redundancy demonstrably misroutes. EXECUTION: both as proper lifecycles (skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md:3 has no bypass; exec Option D rejected); Lifecycle 1 implement via worktree-interactive if the sibling session is confirmed live, else plain feature branch (hazard premise is stale — I verified git status clean and #287 feature_complete); direct orchestrator edits, not Agent dispatch (worktree sub-agent instability memory); Lifecycle 2 plain feature branch on the shared checkout, sequential dispatch, pathspec-scoped commits (editable-install staleness makes worktree verification unreliable for cortex_command). Both produce branch+PR. Non-negotiables in every commit: canonical+regenerated plugins/cortex-core mirror staged together (drift hook is branch-agnostic), kept-pauses inventory + parity-test anchors updated in the same commit as line-shifting edits (LINE_TOLERANCE=35), events-registry/contract/skill-path lints clean, /cortex-core:commit only. SEQUENCING: Lifecycle 2 first or parallel (independent, ~10 lines + tests, fast); within Lifecycle 1, structure-first: T1 dedup extractions, T2 disclosure (b) moves, T3 per-file in-place trims (content-matched, not stale line numbers) with per-file anchor reconciliation, T4 verification sweep — full test suite, all pre-commit gates, post-trim grep of cortex_command/** for §/Step citations into trimmed files (orchestrator-round.md:242/302/413 cites plan.md §1a/§1b normatively; lifecycle_config.py:8-9 cites plan.md §5 and complete.md Step 2 — do not renumber or update citations in-lockstep), before/after token measurement via bin/cortex-count-tokens + cortex-measure-l1-surface recorded in lifecycle artifacts. DEFERRED to backlog: disclosure option (c) dispatch table; frontmatter L1 re-trim (+44% regrowth, 8339B vs 5777B post-#191) with the missing cap-enforcement test; trim maps for research/SKILL.md (15KB) + critical-review references (~45KB) + the 8 unmapped lifecycle references (~24KB); metrics Option C (pure-events reducer in common.py, natural follow-on to #287); guard note on cortex_command/init/templates/cortex/lifecycle.config.md near-copy if assets/ is ever touched.

arguments.claim: DIFFERENCE 1 — the proposal bundles disclosure options (b)+(c) in one pass; I ship (b) only and defer (c). Bundling folds a routing-semantics change with zero byte payoff into what should be a no-behavior-change PR.
arguments.evidence: The evidence's own disclosure recommendation: 'Recommend option (b), with (c)'s single-table formalization as an optional second commit if the two-gate redundancy bothers you in practice' — and (c)'s est_savings states 'Same bytes as (b)... the win over (b) is reliability, not bytes.' (c)'s named risk: the spec-without-research warning at SKILL.md:138 'must carry that warning explicitly or it silently vanishes,' plus a larger Step 2 table rewrite hitting parity anchors. No events.log or transcript evidence shows the existing two-gate redundancy ever misrouting — by the repo's own evidence-before-escalation culture (CLAUDE.md MUST-escalation policy analog), restructure on observed failure, not anticipation. The proposal's bundling rationale ('touch Step 2/3 + parity anchors once instead of twice') is weak economy: the trim+dedup pass touches those anchors regardless, and the second touch may never be needed. Under the user's 'behavior changes need explicit approval' constraint, (c) raises the approval and review burden of the entire PR for zero token gain.

arguments.claim: DIFFERENCE 2 — the proposal bundles the tier-attribution semantics change (re-bucketing 63/237 = 27% of completed features) into the same lifecycle as a ~46KB prose trim; I split it into its own small simple-tier lifecycle/PR.
arguments.evidence: Three concrete reasons. (1) Revert isolation: the user's standing constraint is 'don't degrade harness performance for token savings' — the realistic failure mode is a post-merge behavioral regression in live sessions remedied by reverting the trim PR; bundling means the revert reintroduces the metrics bug. (2) Review-mode conflict: the trim PR's review question is 'verify nothing changed semantically' while the metrics fix's is 'verify this intended semantic change' — a 27% re-bucketing that visibly shifts the SessionStart metrics line (scan_lifecycle.py:505-513) is exactly the kind of change that gets lost in a 12-file prose diff, undermining the explicit-approval constraint even though the spec gate technically provides it. (3) The metricsFix evidence itself recommends 'one simple-tier lifecycle' for the two fixes as a unit, and exec Option C exists precisely for this split ('worktree for skills trim, trunk sequential for cortex_command fixes'); metrics Option A touches only metrics.py + hooks/scan_lifecycle.py — neither in the lifecycle-required path list except by safe-read convention — so the split lifecycle is cheap (~10 lines code + tests). Side-effect: removing the cortex_command driver from the big lifecycle frees its implement mode — the proposal's 'sequential dispatch given cortex_command edits' constraint only exists because of the bundling. Note the proposal's concurrency premise is also stale: I verified the tree is clean and #287 is feature_complete (commit 25782ca4), though the .session file persists.

arguments.claim: DIFFERENCE 3 — the proposal defers the discovery/lifecycle orchestrator-review drift-pair sync while trimming the lifecycle copy now; I include the cheap sync in-scope (or defer that file's trim along with it). Trimming one side of a near-duplicate pair is the worst of both options.
arguments.evidence: Critic gap 3: skills/discovery/references/orchestrator-review.md vs skills/lifecycle/references/orchestrator-review.md differ by only 79 lines across 109/152-line files, 'trimming the lifecycle copy alone entrenches a live cross-tree drift pair,' and 'a sync-or-extract task is cheap' (impact: adds-task). The lifecycle copy IS in the proposal's 12-file trim list (trim[7], ~2.4KB savings), so the proposal actively widens the divergence it defers fixing — every future dedup of this pair gets harder. Applying the equivalent trims to the discovery copy in the same pass is mechanical and keeps the pair diffable; alternatively dropping trim[7] from this pass costs only 2.4KB of 36.5KB.

arguments.claim: DIFFERENCE 4 — the proposal's execution rules omit a post-trim guard for section-number citations from cortex_command code and overnight prompts into the trimmed files; no test covers these, so breakage is silent.
arguments.evidence: Critic gap 6: cortex_command/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md:242,302,413 cite plan.md §1a/§1b as normative precedent for envelope parsing; cortex_command/lifecycle_config.py:8-9,95-96 cite plan.md §5 and complete.md Step 2; report.py:965 cites review.md §4a; tests/test_lifecycle_references_resolve.py only validates citations originating from cortex/research/**, not cortex_command/**. The disclosure summary's 'overnight runner is unaffected' holds for SKILL.md restructuring but NOT for plan.md/review.md section renumbering. The plan needs one rule: preserve §1a/§1b/§5/Step-2/§4a numbering in plan.md, review.md, complete.md, and run a post-trim grep of cortex_command/** for §/Step citations into every trimmed file. Cheap to add; silent-failure class if omitted.

arguments.claim: AGREEMENTS and minor deltas — the proposal is otherwise sound: lifecycle-not-bypass, verified-safe-only trim scope (36.5KB matches safeTotalBytes), dedup inventory and skips, metrics A+D+E semantics, kept-pauses same-commit discipline, canonical+mirror co-commit, and the deferral of frontmatter/research/critical-review trim maps all match my independent plan. Two minor explicitness gaps remain.
arguments.evidence: (1) The proposal should state that the 37 downgrade_to verdicts ship in downgraded form and the 36 unsafe verdicts are excluded entirely — '36.5KB verified-safe' implies but does not state this, and several downgrades carry mandatory co-edits (e.g. implement.md TOC removal requires the SKILL.md:219 inventory anchor update in the same commit; complete.md Python-block removal requires extending line 53 with the criterion clause). (2) The proposal's deferral list omits the 8 unmapped lifecycle reference files (~24KB, critic gap 5) and the init-template lifecycle.config.md near-copy guard (critic gap 7) — both belong on the deferred ticket list so the next pass starts from the full inventory.
